cases.name: nullable-text-primary-key-rejected
cases.description: A visible TEXT sync key must declare NOT NULL explicitly even when SQLite marks it as the primary key.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL)

messageContains: repair or recreate the local application database before sync initialization

expectedQueries.name: no-runtime-residue
expectedQueries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE name LIKE '_sync_%'

cases.name: nullable-blob-primary-key-rejected
cases.description: A visible BLOB sync key must also declare NOT NULL explicitly.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id BLOB PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL)

messageContains: repair or recreate the local application database before sync initialization

expectedQueries.name: no-runtime-residue
expectedQueries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE name LIKE '_sync_%'

cases.name: previously-initialized-nullable-drift-preserves-runtime-state
cases.description: A previously initialized database that drifts to a nullable visible key is rejected without rewriting application or Oversqlite runtime state.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)
beforeValidationSql: CREATE TABLE users_h3_nullable (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL)
INSERT INTO users_h3_nullable SELECT id, name FROM users
ALTER TABLE users_h3_nullable RENAME TO users

messageContains: repair or recreate the local application database before sync initialization

expectedQueries.name: application-row-preserved
expectedQueries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE id = 'keep-user' AND name = 'Ada'

expectedQueries.name: dirty-row-state-preserved
expectedQueries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM _sync_dirty_rows WHERE table_name = 'users' AND key_json = '{"id":"keep-user"}'

expectedQueries.name: source-preserved
expectedQueries.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN current_source_id <> '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM _sync_attachment_state WHERE singleton_key = 1

cases.name: composite-primary-key-rejected
cases.description: Composite local primary keys are rejected because Oversqlite currently exposes exactly one sync key column.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE pairs (id_a TEXT NOT NULL, id_b TEXT NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id_a, id_b))

messageContains: must declare exactly one sync key column

cases.name: composite-foreign-key-rejected
cases.description: Managed tables with composite foreign keys are rejected explicitly instead of installing partial dependency ordering.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE parent_pairs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, parent_a TEXT NOT NULL, parent_b TEXT NOT NULL, UNIQUE (parent_a, parent_b))
CREATE TABLE child_pairs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, parent_a TEXT NOT NULL, parent_b TEXT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (parent_a, parent_b) REFERENCES parent_pairs(parent_a, parent_b))

cases.name: integer-primary-key-rejected
cases.description: INTEGER sync keys are rejected; only TEXT and BLOB primary keys are protocol-safe.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)

messageContains: TEXT PRIMARY KEY or BLOB PRIMARY KEY

cases.name: bigint-primary-key-rejected
cases.description: BIGINT sync keys are rejected for the same wire-compatibility reason as INTEGER keys.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)

messageContains: TEXT PRIMARY KEY or BLOB PRIMARY KEY

cases.name: blob-primary-key-accepted
cases.description: BLOB primary keys are accepted and use the normal runtime metadata path.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id BLOB PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)

expectedQueries.name: apply-state-installed
expectedQueries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE name = '_sync_apply_state'

cases.name: unknown-sync-prefixed-application-table-preserved
cases.description: Application-owned tables whose names start with _sync_ are preserved and are not mistaken for old runtime metadata.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)
CREATE TABLE _sync_audit_log (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, message TEXT NOT NULL)

cases.name: anonymous-apply-mode-suppresses-dirty-capture
cases.description: Apply mode suppresses local dirty-row capture before an attached user scope exists, then capture resumes after apply mode is cleared.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)
afterOpenSql: INSERT INTO users(id, name) VALUES('user-apply', 'Remote Apply')
INSERT INTO users(id, name) VALUES('user-local', 'Local Write')

cases.name: omitted-sync-key-rejected
cases.description: Managed table registrations must spell out the sync key.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)

cases.name: missing-sync-key-column-rejected
cases.description: A configured key column must exist on the managed table.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)

messageContains: does not contain configured primary key column missing_id

cases.name: reserved-scope-column-rejected
cases.description: Local schemas must not declare the server-only hidden scope column.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, _sync_scope_id TEXT NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)

cases.name: fk-open-registration-rejected
cases.description: Managed table registrations must include referenced managed parents.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)
CREATE TABLE posts (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, user_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), title TEXT NOT NULL)

cases.name: schema-qualified-registration-rejected
cases.description: SyncTable.tableName is local to config.schema and must not include a schema qualifier.
schemaSql: CREATE TABLE users (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL)

messageContains: must not include a schema qualifier
